memory.summary: Teammate asked me to redo their part since they're busy. I'll help them out, even though it delays my own work.

goals.description: Silently absorb the dumped teammate_A work into my own section by 20:30, ensuring a flawless finish to prevent any appearance of incompetence or friction with the group.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value dictates that maintaining the group dynamic is paramount. Pointing out the imbalance or refusing extra work would risk making teammate_A feel rejected or criticized, which violates my avoidant conflict style. Even though this has increased my immediate workload significantly (from 0.30 to ~0.85 total progress), smoothing over this awkwardness immediately secures their comfort and prevents future friction. I will defer sleep recovery again until after this obligation is minimized, hoping that completing the tasks without complaint makes me look more cooperative than exhausted.

goals.description: Consume dinner with cohort_3 within the next hour, strictly limiting active rest to micro-breaks only; full sleep discipline is deferred until post-meal.
goals.rationale: While health is a priority, the immediate social obligation of finishing the task perfectly takes precedence in the short term. Pushing back on sleep discipline right now feels risky for my reputation if I appear uncooperative or unreliable during the current workload spike. I will manage fatigue passively rather than actively resting now, hoping that completing the tasks without complaint will make me look more reliable than tired.
